response.justification: Bogdan brings nearly 20 years of broad individual-contributor experience with genuine relevance to developer tooling, CI/CD infrastructure, and test automation frameworks — areas central to the role's engineering productivity focus. However, the position is explicitly a people-management role requiring 2+ years of managing direct reports and 3+ years of technical leadership, neither of which is evidenced anywhere in the resume. His entire career has been as a Senior or Staff IC with no mention of direct reports, hiring, performance cycles, or team headcount ownership. Knowledge-sharing activities such as Refactoring Dojos and Selenium courses demonstrate a mentorship inclination but do not satisfy the management minimum.
strengths: Hands-on developer tooling and infrastructure experience — CI servers, custom test runners (Trial), MCP code generators, and automated pipelines — maps directly to the engineering productivity charter
Nearly two decades of technical depth across web, mobile, embedded, and systems programming provides the credibility needed to guide and review engineers on complex designs

Consistent pattern of knowledge-sharing through conference talks, full-day workshops, and internal Refactoring Dojos signals team-development instincts that could translate to management with the right growth path
concerns: Zero people management evidence: no mention of direct reports, hiring, performance reviews, headcount planning, or formal team leadership with accountability for others' growth
No mobile platform depth: the role targets a major mobile ecosystem, and his only mobile exposure is a brief iOS/Apple TV contribution at WatchUp in 2015–2016

No organizational leadership or strategic roadmap ownership at scale: the closest analog is sole-founder work on an open-source project (GISCollective), which does not meet the 3-year organizational change requirement
